phrasal_verbs.meaning: if sth grows on you, you start to like it more
give sb or sth the same name as sb or sth else
prevent a sound from being heard by making a louder noise
believe that a trick or joke is true
try to attract people's attention and make them admire you (usually used negatively)
look or behave like an older relative
be remembered for having done something
become successful or popular very fast

part: have a part (in a play, etc)
play: have a part/role to play (in sth)

F.title: Original key word transformation practice
F.instructions: Complete the second sentence so that it keeps the same meaning as the first.

questions.sentence:
Mia began the class project before the lesson on Entertainment. **YOURSELF**
The class project ...... before the lesson on Entertainment.

Leo began the study group before the lesson on Entertainment. **BOUND**
The study group ...... before the lesson on Entertainment.

Sara began the exam workshop before the lesson on Entertainment. **INSTEAD**
The exam workshop ...... before the lesson on Entertainment.

Tom began the online lesson before the lesson on Entertainment. **AVOID**
The online lesson ...... before the lesson on Entertainment.
questions.answer: avoid queuing by getting/avoid the queue by getting

questions.sentence:
Nina began the school club before the lesson on Entertainment. **APOLOGISED**
The school club ...... before the lesson on Entertainment.
questions.answer: apologised for not inviting/apologised for not having invited
